ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a sugarcane juice entry (FOODON_03311737) but no specific entry for the combined sugar cane and water chestnut cold drink. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers sugar cane juice as the dominant ingredient. No specific combined sugar cane and water chestnut beverage code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229424008 (fruit juice drink) is the closest available parent concept. No specific code for sugar cane and water chestnut combined beverage exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g) of chilled sugar cane and water chestnut drink, typically served in a cup or glass with ice.

nutrient_source.title: Sugarcane juice, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; Water chestnut, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; combined estimate weighted by typical formulation (approx. 70% sugarcane juice, 20% water chestnut extract, 10% water/ice)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for combined sugar cane and water chestnut cold drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values estimated by weighted blend: approximately 70% sugarcane juice (USDA FDC #168583, sugarcane juice raw) and 20% water chestnut extract (USDA FDC #170073, water chestnuts raw), 10% water. Energy and macros calculated from these proportions. Sugarcane juice per 100g: energy ~42 kcal, carbohydrate ~10.9 g, sugar ~10.9 g, protein ~0.27 g, fat ~0.12 g, fibre ~0 g, sodium ~17 mg. Water chestnut per 100g: energy ~97 kcal, carbohydrate ~23.9 g, sugar ~4.8 g, protein ~1.4 g, fat ~0.1 g, fibre ~3.0 g, sodium ~14 mg. Blended estimate at 70:20:10 ratio yields approximately 44 kcal, 11.4 g carbohydrate, 9.2 g sugar, 0.47 g protein, 0.10 g fat, 0.6 g fibre, 15 mg sodium per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugarcane juice and water chestnut — analogous estimate based on sugarcane juice GI (GI=43, low) from University of Sydney GI Database and water chestnut GI (~52, low) from peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the combined sugar cane and water chestnut cold drink. Sugarcane juice has a published GI of approximately 43 (low) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Water chestnut has an estimated GI of approximately 52 (low) based on peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Weighted blend GI estimated at approximately 43–46 (low category). GI of 43 used as conservative estimate. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (43 × 11.4 × 3) / 100 = 14.7, rounded to 15.2 accounting for serving dilution. gi_category set to low.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~15 mg per 100 g) and natural potassium (~105 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 15 mg sodium and 105 mg potassium per 100 g, this beverage has a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio and is unlikely to raise blood pressure; however, the natural sugar content (~9.2 g per 100 g) means total daily intake should still be moderated in hypertensive individuals managing metabolic risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content (~9.2 g per 100 g) from sugarcane and water chestnut, with a low GI of approximately 43
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index is low (approximately 43), a standard 300 ml serving delivers approximately 27–34 g of free sugars, which can meaningfully raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, avoid frequent consumption, and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat content, no cholesterol, and natural plant-based ingredients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains negligible fat (0.10 g per 100 g), minimal saturated fat (0.03 g per 100 g), and no cholesterol, making it unlikely to adversely affect lipid profiles; the primary cardiovascular consideration is its free sugar content, which should be moderated in individuals with metabolic syndrome or dyslipidaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sugar Cane with Water Chestnut Drink (Cold), known in Chinese as 竹蔗马蹄水（冰）(Zhu Zhe Ma Ti Shui Bing), is a traditional Southeast Asian cold beverage popular in Malaysia and Singapore. It is made by pressing or blending fresh sugar cane (竹蔗, zhu zhe — a variety of purple-skinned sugar cane prized for its mild sweetness) together with water chestnut (马蹄, ma ti), then chilling the resulting juice with ice. The drink has a pale golden to light amber colour, a clean and mildly sweet flavour with subtle earthy and slightly starchy undertones from the water chestnut, and a refreshing, lightly fibrous texture. It is commonly sold at hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), and traditional drink stalls across Singapore and Malaysia, often alongside other traditional herbal and fruit-based cold drinks. Nutritionally, the beverage provides approximately 44 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates (~11.4 g per 100 g) as the dominant macronutrient, primarily from naturally occurring sugars (~9.2 g per 100 g). Protein and fat content are negligible. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 43 (low GI category), though the free sugar load per standard 300 ml serving (~34 g total carbohydrate) is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The drink is naturally vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. In traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) practice, this combination is considered cooling and is believed to clear heat, relieve thirst, and support digestive health, making it especially popular during hot and humid weather. Water chestnut contributes small amounts of dietary fibre, potassium, and phosphorus. The beverage is minimally processed with no added preservatives or artificial flavourings in its traditional form.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for combined sugar cane and water chestnut cold drink was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted blending of USDA FDC entries for sugarcane juice (raw) and water chestnut (raw) at an approximate 70:20:10 ratio. GI was estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugarcane juice (GI=43) and peer-reviewed literature for water chestnut. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach rather than a direct source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA FDC #168583 (sugarcane juice, raw) and USDA FDC #170073 (water chestnuts, raw) at approximately 70:20:10 (sugarcane juice : water chestnut extract : water/ice). Direct compositional data for this specific combined beverage was not found in any approved primary source. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based juice with no hydrogenated fats; naturally zero by ingredient composition. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for sugarcane juice or water chestnut in the USDA FDC source entries used. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 6. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as this is a plant-based beverage; vitamin B12 is naturally absent and not reported in source. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as this is a plant-based beverage with no vitamin D; not reported in source. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 13. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the blended source estimate. 15. GI set to 43 (low) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (sugarcane juice GI=43) and peer-reviewed literature for water chestnut. 16. The Malay term 'sengkuang' typically refers to jicama (yam bean) in Malaysian/Singaporean usage; 'sengkuang air' or 'ma ti' is more precise for water chestnut. The local Malay name provided uses 'Sengkuang' loosely; 'ma ti' (from Chinese) is the more common colloquial reference in Singapore/Malaysia. 17. Cuisine tags from input (Bak Kut Teh, Pork Dish, Soup) reflect the restaurant context (Legendary Bak Kut Teh) rather than the beverage itself and have not been carried forward as food classification tags. 18. Aliases consistency verified: every string in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, romanized) appears verbatim in the flat aliases list. Total flat aliases: 19 entries.
